title: Outdoor Adventures and Environmental Protection Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: To accept or admit that something is true, or to recognise that you have received something.
choices: acknowledge
demonstrate
establish
rounds.answer: acknowledge
rounds.explanation: acknowledge: To accept or admit that something is true, or to recognise that you have received something.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: To communicate or express a message, feeling or idea.
choices: acknowledge
demonstrate
convey
rounds.answer: convey
rounds.explanation: convey: To communicate or express a message, feeling or idea.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: To keep something going or to support it over a long period of time.
choices: demonstrate
sustain
acknowledge
rounds.answer: sustain
rounds.explanation: sustain: To keep something going or to support it over a long period of time.

choices: Important or meaningful; large enough to have an effect.

rounds.explanation: Losing so many trees was a significant blow to the local ecosystem.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'obliterate' mean in this unit?
choices: To completely destroy or remove something so it no longer exists.
To accept or admit that something is true, or to recognise that you have received something.
To show how something works or to prove something clearly.
rounds.answer: To completely destroy or remove something so it no longer exists.
rounds.explanation: obliterate: To completely destroy or remove something so it no longer exists.

rounds.prompt: What does 'admonish' mean in this unit?
choices: To accept or admit that something is true, or to recognise that you have received something.
To show how something works or to prove something clearly.
To tell someone firmly that they have done something wrong, often in a caring way.
rounds.answer: To tell someone firmly that they have done something wrong, often in a caring way.
rounds.explanation: admonish: To tell someone firmly that they have done something wrong, often in a caring way.

rounds.prompt: What does 'demonstrate' mean in this unit?
choices: To create or start something that is meant to last.
To show how something works or to prove something clearly.
To accept or admit that something is true, or to recognise that you have received something.
rounds.answer: To show how something works or to prove something clearly.
rounds.explanation: demonstrate: To show how something works or to prove something clearly.

games.title: Memory Pairs

To make something less severe, harmful or serious.
To tell someone firmly that they have done something wrong, often in a caring way.
To copy someone or something in order to be like them.
To give up or leave something or someone behind.
